mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll gains above 150k signal robust labor market demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 60%+ of months with PAYEMS > 150k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 70%+ of instances with FEDFUNDS < 1.5%, or IC < 0.01 in 2-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation and falling Fed funds rates create stagflation risks, causing S&P 500 20d returns to turn negative as investors price in policy uncertainty.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances with CPIAUCSL > 3% and FEDFUNDS < 2%, or interaction effect IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts above 1.8M units signal strong demand for construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 50%+ of HOUST > 1.8M periods, or IC < 0.02 in 5-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production growth above 1.2% indicates strong supply-side capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 55%+ of INDPRO > 1.2% months, or IC < 0.025 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in both consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening demand, causing S&P 500 5d returns to turn negative.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances with RSAFS < 0.5% and HOUST < 0.3%, or interaction effect IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate below 3.5% signals tight labor market conditions, driving wage inflation and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 55%+ of UNRATE < 3.5% periods, or IC < 0.02 in 4-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDP growth above 3% combined with falling consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates demand-side imbalances, causing S&P 500 60d returns to turn negative.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 50%+ of instances with GDPC1 > 3% and RSAFS < 0.2%, or interaction effect IC < 0.015.
